title: INPATIENT AND EMERGENCY SERVICES - 211info
text: UNITY CENTER FOR BEHAVIORAL HEALTH INPATIENT AND EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides psychiatric inpatient services for youth and adults, and a Psychiatric Emergency Service (PES). On-site representatives from various community partners and peer support personnel offer transitional support services. 1225 NE 2nd Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Inpatient: Ages 9-70 PES: Age 18 and older Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 944-8000 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 944-8000 Crisis line (503) 988-4888 Multnomah County Crisis line (503) 655-8585 Clackamas County Crisis line (503) 291-9111 Washington County Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in for emergency services. Patients may also be referred by a hospital emergency department. FEES: Accepts OHP and most private insurances. Financial assistance is available and no one will be turned away due to inability to pay. DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, if applicable LANGUAGES: English, limited interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Near TriMet bus lines 6, 8, 17, 77, and Portland Streetcar A Loop Services: In Person Crisis Intervention Inpatient Mental Health Facilities Psychiatric Emergency Room Care If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. UNITY CENTER FOR BEHAVIORAL HEALTH
